summary: Fujiwa polling orchestration (#298): run_fujiwa_poll_cycle wires production-read credential resolution, token refresh, orders/products/returns sync with rate-limit backoff, and tiktok_sync_state persistence. All three acceptance criteria mapped to tests; 35 unit tests pass; ruff clean. Refactor pass verified (_PollStep loop, batched repo save).
recommendations: Wire run_fujiwa_poll_cycle to Celery beat when worker infra lands.
Run migration 009_tiktok_sync_state before first production poll cycle.
Add cycle-start/cycle-complete structured logs when observability slice lands.

maintainabilityFindings.description: _RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS (10) in orchestrate.py duplicates sync.py acquire limits; acceptable until shared polling constants module exists.

securityFindings.description: resolve_credential is injectable for tests; production wiring must use default resolve_production_read_credential — guard still validates returned credential.

architectureFindings.description: Sync cursors persisted once at cycle end; mid-cycle crash is safe (re-fetch from last committed cursor) but may repeat work.

observabilityFindings.description: rate_limit_backoff logged per endpoint; no cycle-level start/complete log yet — deferred to worker wiring slice.
